whyToday: This unit is P0 for target score 200.
The task order follows concept -> basic -> standard -> transfer -> review.

tasks.goal: Partial-score training starts from data ranges: brute force for small data, special properties for middle score, full solution last.
beforeCodingQuestions: Can I code the partial route now?
When should I stop chasing full marks?

tasks.goal: Finish the standard form with low pressure.
tasks.passSignal: For 5 T3/T4 tasks, write 30/50/70 routes before coding.

tasks.goal: Handle boundary, complexity and submission stability.
tasks.passSignal: Record result, submissions, hint usage and failed stage.

tasks.goal: Verify transfer instead of memorizing the original problem.
tasks.passSignal: Solve without using hint level >= 3 and explain what changed from the base problem.

tasks.goal: Record result, score, submissions, hint level, failed stage and next redo decision.

passCriteria: For 5 T3/T4 tasks, write 30/50/70 routes before coding.
Do not use full solution before attempt.
Record actual score vs predicted route.
scoreRouteReminder: First protect T1 and T2. A 200-point route cannot tolerate unstable low-level mistakes.
Then train T3 partial-score decomposition. The goal is executable 30/50/70 routes, not immediate full marks.
Use T4 only as strategy training unless T1/T2 are already stable.
Every week must include one CSP-S-style set to verify transfer from single-problem practice to exam scoring.
